design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally scrolling stages while managing three weapon types, dodging enemy fire, and capturing enemies via Seizer Beam to use as shields or projectiles against incoming threats
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (weapon variety, lives) balanced against escalating enemy bullet patterns and stage difficulty; maintaining firepower through pickups while avoiding permanent loss
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot the ZIG spacecraft as Trent to overthrow the alien space pirate organization CATS and liberate Earth from occupation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The Seizer Beam mechanic creates a unique defensive-offensive gameplay layer rarely seen in traditional shmups—enemies become tools rather than just threats. Weapon switching (Red Cannon/Blue Laser/Green Homing) forces tactical choices about upgrade paths. Game prioritizes firepower/speed degradation on hit over instant death, creating resource tension rather than pattern memorization alone.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; getting hit by enemy fire or stage obstacles costs a life and resets firepower/speed to original state
difficulty.continues_system: Insert-more-credits continuation system (arcade standard); home versions feature checkpoint or respawn options depending on settings
difficulty.checkpointing: Configurable in arcade version: either checkpoint-based (restart at last checkpoint reached) or respawn-based (restart at death location); resets firepower/speed penalties on death regardless
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate agency through weapon selection strategy (three switchable weapon types with independent upgrade paths) and Seizer Beam tactical use; limited difficulty settings mentioned but not detailed

progression.upgrade_system: Three-weapon upgrade system: Red Cannon, Blue Laser, Green Homing missiles. Each weapon can be upgraded independently by collecting same-colored pickups. Firepower/speed multipliers available as drop items. Bomb module adds destructive utility.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight mandatory stages with boss gates; stages unlock sequentially. Game loops back to Stage 1 after completion with increased difficulty (denser bullet patterns, extra bullets on enemy destruction).
replayability_hooks: Arcade loops with escalating difficulty encourage score chasing and pattern mastery
Three weapon types create different playstyle variations across runs
Seizer Beam mechanics reward creative enemy manipulation strategies
Configurable checkpoint/respawn systems alter challenge pacing

world_building_techniques: Introductory cutscene establishing conflict (notably infamous for 'All your base are belong to us' mistranslation in EU Mega Drive version)
Stage progression through space environments (Japanese/Federation colonies) implies ongoing military campaign
Protagonist motivation (avenge mothership, liberate Earth) frames each boss encounter as tactical objective
Multiple narrative versions across ports: Arcade/Genesis feature CATS vs. PC-Engine's Abigor organization with romantic subplot

boss_design.philosophy: Mandatory boss gates at each stage end create hard progression walls; bosses escalate in complexity alongside stage enemies. Boss patterns correlate with stage number difficulty.

faction_indicators: CATS: alien space pirate organization (primary antagonist)
Abigor: rival organization in PC-Engine version led by Ludwig and Seiren
United Nations/Milky Way Federation: player-aligned authority
ZIG pilot protagonist (Trent/Masato Tachibana) as liberation symbol
danger_signals: CATS breaks peace treaty and occupies space colonies (2101 setting)
Mothership destruction signals escalating military threat
Stage bosses represent CATS command hierarchy
Denser bullet patterns and increased enemy spawns indicate organizational strength
environmental_storytelling: Horizontally scrolling stages traverse contested territory (Japanese/Federation space colonies under occupation)
Stage environments shift from colonized space to deeper enemy territory as progression advances
Enemy carrier types and formations suggest CATS military structure and resource deployment
